remember: Students will be able to recall the concept of Globalisation and its various dimensions.
Students will be able to identify and explain the different types of globalisation.
Students will be able to name the key features of Globalisation.
understand: Students will be able to comprehend the causes and consequences of Globalisation.
Students will be able to explain how Globalisation affects different countries and societies.
Students will be able to understand the role of Globalisation in shaping international relations.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of Globalisation to real-world scenarios and case studies.
Students will be able to analyse the impact of Globalisation on different economic, political and social systems.
Students will be able to evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of Globalisation.
analyze: Students will be able to critically analyse the effects of Globalisation on local cultures and identities.
Students will be able to examine the relationship between Globalisation and inequality.
Students will be able to assess the role of Globalisation in shaping global governance and institutions.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, students should be able to understand the meaning and dimensions of Globalisation.
Students should be able to explain the causes and consequences of Globalisation.
Students should be able to identify the key features and types of Globalisation.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE tests students' understanding of the concept of Globalisation and its impact on international relations and economies.
Students are assessed on their ability to apply the concept of Globalisation to real-world scenarios and case studies.
CBSE evaluates students' understanding of the benefits and drawbacks of Globalisation and its effects on local cultures and identities.
